questions.question: What is the primary function of a consensus algorithm in a blockchain?
questions.explanation: A consensus algorithm is used to secure and verify transactions on the blockchain, ensuring that all nodes agree on the state of the network.

questions.question: What is the term for a record of all transactions made on the blockchain?
questions.explanation: A transaction log, also known as a blockchain, is a record of all transactions made on the network, stored in blocks and linked together.

questions.question: What is the name of the Ethereum client that uses proof-of-work consensus?
questions.explanation: Parity is an Ethereum client that uses proof-of-work consensus, also known as PoW, to secure and verify transactions on the network.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a smart contract on the Ethereum blockchain?
questions.explanation: Smart contracts are self-executing contracts with the terms of the agreement written directly into lines of code, executed on the blockchain when conditions are met.

questions.question: What is the term for the process of validating and adding new blocks to the blockchain?
questions.explanation: Mining is the process of validating and adding new blocks to the blockchain, requiring specialized computers to solve complex mathematical problems.
